releaseNotes: v0.38.1 — Scanning and reliability fixes. Manufacturer 2D cards that scanned on the main page but failed inside a zone audit now scan everywhere (some cards' barcodes omit the standard field separators, which was being treated as a hard error instead of a harmless warning) — this also applies to Daily Intake and pull/dispense scanning. If an older copy of RxRescue ever opens a database a newer version created, you now get a clear 'you're running an outdated copy — update' screen with your data confirmed safe, instead of a cryptic database error. You can now import a Liberty stock report straight from the Zone Audit screen (per zone or a whole group). Accessibility: screen readers announce expiry-badge urgency, the selected sort, and every button/tooltip; full app-wide localization groundwork. Liberty CSV files saved from Windows with accented drug names no longer fail to import. Under the hood, the Windows installer is hardened so a release can never package an outdated build. The auto-updater rolls 0.38.0 -> 0.38.1 on next launch; your data is unchanged. 787 tests passing; three-reviewer pre-release audit, no blocking findings.
